notes: Chapter is overwhelmingly theological/eschatological narrative of events White presents as future (post-millennial second resurrection, final judgment, destruction of the wicked, the earth made new). Per CLAUDE.md rule 5, all supernatural/theological/prophetic-interpretation assertions are logged as claim_type theological (or interpretive for sweeping judgments) with needs_verification false; they are not empirically checkable and are recorded only for centrality analysis. The chapter is unusually quote-dense: nearly every paragraph from ¶3 onward closes with a footnoted scripture citation (footnotes 1157-1194 in the source 1911 edition). All such quotations are extracted as claim_type attributed_quote with needs_verification true, since quote fidelity to the KJV text and correct book/chapter attribution are checkable regardless of the passages' theological content. A small cluster of claims embeds ordinary historical/biographical propositions inside the vision narrative (Herod and the Massacre of the Innocents, Herodias and John the Baptist, Jesus' trial sequence before Annas/Pilate/Herod, Nero's persecution of Christians and his mother's influence, medieval Catholic use of torture/imprisonment/burning) — these are flagged needs_verification true as ordinary checkable historical claims, independent of the surrounding theological frame. No paragraph produced zero claims.

claims.quote: At the close of the thousand years, Christ again returns to the earth.
claims.claim_text: Christ returns to earth a second time at the close of the millennium (the thousand years), per White's eschatology.

claims.quote: He is accompanied by the host of the redeemed, and attended by a retinue of angels.
claims.claim_text: At His post-millennial return, Christ is accompanied by the redeemed and a retinue of angels.

claims.quote: As He descends in terrific majesty, He bids the wicked dead arise to receive their doom. They come forth, a mighty host, numberless as the sands of the sea.
claims.claim_text: At Christ's post-millennial return, the wicked dead are resurrected in a vast, numberless host to receive judgment (the second resurrection).

claims.quote: The righteous were clothed with immortal youth and beauty. The wicked bear the traces of disease and death.
claims.claim_text: In the resurrection scenario described, the righteous appear clothed with immortal youth and beauty, while the resurrected wicked bear the marks of disease and death.

claims.quote: With one voice the wicked hosts exclaim, “Blessed is He that cometh in the name of the Lord!”
claims.claim_text: The resurrected wicked are depicted exclaiming in unison 'Blessed is He that cometh in the name of the Lord,' a phrase echoing scripture (cf. Psalm 118:26, Matthew 21:9, 23:39).
claims.extractor_note: Verify verbatim wording against KJV Ps 118:26 / Matt 23:39; the speaker attribution ('the wicked hosts') is White's narrative framing, not the original biblical speaker.

claims.quote: It is not love to Jesus that inspires this utterance. The force of truth urges the words from unwilling lips.
claims.claim_text: White interprets the wicked's acclamation of Christ as compelled by the force of truth rather than by love for Him.

claims.quote: They are to have no new probation, in which to remedy the defects of their past lives.
claims.claim_text: According to White's theology, the resurrected wicked are given no second probation to amend their past lives.

claims.quote: Christ descends upon the Mount of Olives, whence, after His resurrection, He ascended, and where angels repeated the promise of His return.
claims.claim_text: Jesus Christ ascended to heaven from the Mount of Olives after His resurrection, and angels there promised His return (cf. Acts 1:9-12).

claims.quote: Says the prophet: “The Lord my God shall come, and all the saints with Thee.” “And His feet shall stand in that day upon the Mount of Olives, which is before Jerusalem on the east, and the Mount of Olives shall cleave in the midst thereof, ... and there shall be a very great valley.” “And the Lord shall be king over all the earth: in that day shall there be one Lord, and His name one.”(1157)
claims.claim_text: Zechariah is quoted (footnote 1157) prophesying that God will come with the saints, that His feet will stand on the Mount of Olives east of Jerusalem causing it to cleave and form a valley, and that the Lord will be king over all the earth (cf. Zechariah 14:4-9).

claims.quote: As the New Jerusalem, in its dazzling splendor, comes down out of heaven, it rests upon the place purified and made ready to receive it, and Christ, with His people and the angels, enters the holy city.
claims.claim_text: The New Jerusalem descends from heaven and rests on the purified earth, whereupon Christ, His people, and the angels enter the holy city.

claims.quote: Now Satan prepares for a last mighty struggle for the supremacy.
claims.claim_text: After the resurrection of the wicked, Satan prepares for a final struggle for supremacy over the earth, per White's narrative.

claims.quote: He will marshal all the armies of the lost under his banner, and through them endeavor to execute his plans.
claims.claim_text: Satan marshals the armies of the resurrected wicked under his banner to execute his final plans.

claims.quote: He claims to be the prince who is the rightful owner of the world, and whose inheritance has been unlawfully wrested from him. He represents himself to his deluded subjects as a redeemer, assuring them that his power has brought them forth from their graves, and that he is about to rescue them from the most cruel tyranny.
claims.claim_text: In White's narrative, Satan falsely claims to be the rightful owner of the world and a redeemer who resurrected the wicked and will rescue them from tyranny.

claims.quote: In that vast throng are multitudes of the long-lived race that existed before the flood; men of lofty stature and giant intellect, who, yielding to the control of fallen angels, devoted all their skill and knowledge to the exaltation of themselves
claims.claim_text: Among the resurrected wicked are the antediluvian 'long-lived race' of great stature and intellect, who came under the control of fallen angels before the Flood.
claims.extractor_note: Contains an embedded historical claim about pre-Flood humans (longevity, giant stature) but is framed entirely as part of the eschatological vision; logged as theological per instructions rather than as an independently asserted historical/biographical fact.

claims.quote: There are kings and generals who conquered nations, valiant men who never lost a battle, proud, ambitious warriors whose approach made kingdoms tremble.
claims.claim_text: Among the resurrected wicked are historical kings, generals, and warriors renowned for conquest.

claims.quote: Satan consults with his angels, and then with these kings and conquerors and mighty men.
claims.claim_text: Satan consults with his angels and with the resurrected kings and conquerors to plan an assault on the New Jerusalem.

claims.quote: All immediately begin to prepare for battle. Skilful artisans construct implements of war. Military leaders, famed for their success, marshal the throngs of warlike men into companies and divisions.
claims.claim_text: The resurrected wicked and Satan's forces prepare for battle, constructing weapons of war and organizing into military divisions.

claims.quote: At last the order to advance is given, and the countless host moves on,—an army such as was never summoned by earthly conquerors, such as the combined forces of all ages since war began on earth could never equal.
claims.claim_text: Satan leads a resurrected army described as larger than the combined armies of all human history, advancing on the New Jerusalem.

claims.quote: By command of Jesus, the gates of the New Jerusalem are closed, and the armies of Satan surround the city, and make ready for the onset.
claims.claim_text: By Christ's command, the gates of the New Jerusalem are closed and Satan's armies surround the city preparing for attack.

claims.quote: Far above the city, upon a foundation of burnished gold, is a throne, high and lifted up. Upon this throne sits the Son of God, and around Him are the subjects of His kingdom.
claims.claim_text: Christ appears enthroned above the city of God, visible to His enemies, at the final judgment scene.

claims.quote: the “great multitude, which no man could number, of all nations, and kindreds, and people, and tongues, ... before the throne, and before the Lamb, clothed with white robes, and palms in their hands.”(1158)
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted (footnote 1158) describing a great multitude of all nations before the throne and the Lamb, clothed in white robes with palms in their hands (cf. Revelation 7:9).

claims.quote: Nearest the throne are those who were once zealous in the cause of Satan, but who, plucked as brands from the burning, have followed their Saviour with deep, intense devotion.
claims.claim_text: In White's vision, those positioned nearest Christ's throne include former zealous followers of Satan who were converted, along with the persecuted righteous and martyrs of all ages.

claims.quote: The redeemed raise a song of praise that echoes and re-echoes through the vaults of heaven, “Salvation to our God which sitteth upon the throne, and unto the Lamb.”
claims.claim_text: The redeemed are depicted singing 'Salvation to our God which sitteth upon the throne, and unto the Lamb,' echoing Revelation 7:10.

claims.quote: In all that shining throng there are none to ascribe salvation to themselves, as if they had prevailed by their own power and goodness.
claims.claim_text: According to White, none among the redeemed multitude claim credit for their own salvation.

claims.quote: In the presence of the assembled inhabitants of earth and heaven the final coronation of the Son of God takes place.
claims.claim_text: The final coronation of Christ takes place in the presence of the assembled inhabitants of earth and heaven, per White's eschatology.

claims.quote: Says the prophet of God: “I saw a great white throne, and Him that sat on it, from whose face the earth and the heaven fled away; and there was found no place for them. And I saw the dead, small and great, stand before God; and the books were opened: and another book was opened, which is the book of life: and the dead were judged out of those things which were written in the books, according to their works.”(1159)
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted (footnote 1159, attributed to 'the prophet of God') describing a great white throne judgment where the dead are judged from opened books, including the book of life, according to their works (cf. Revelation 20:11-12).

claims.quote: As soon as the books of record are opened, and the eye of Jesus looks upon the wicked, they are conscious of every sin which they have ever committed.
claims.claim_text: At judgment, the wicked become conscious of every sin they have committed once the books of record are opened, per White's theology.

claims.quote: The Saviour’s lowly birth; His early life of simplicity and obedience; His baptism in Jordan; the fast and temptation in the wilderness
claims.claim_text: Jesus Christ was born in humble circumstances, lived a life of simplicity and obedience, was baptized in the Jordan River, and subsequently fasted and was tempted in the wilderness.

claims.quote: the awful, mysterious agony in Gethsemane, beneath the crushing weight of the sins of the whole world; His betrayal into the hands of the murderous mob; the fearful events of that night of horror,—the unresisting prisoner, forsaken by His best-loved disciples, rudely hurried through the streets of Jerusalem
claims.claim_text: Jesus Christ suffered agony in Gethsemane, was betrayed, and was seized and hurried through the streets of Jerusalem, forsaken by His disciples.

claims.quote: the Son of God exultingly displayed before Annas, arraigned in the high priest’s palace, in the judgment-hall of Pilate, before the cowardly and cruel Herod, mocked, insulted, tortured, and condemned to die
claims.claim_text: Jesus Christ was arraigned before Annas, tried in the high priest's palace, brought before Pontius Pilate in his judgment hall, and sent before Herod, where He was mocked, insulted, and condemned to death.

claims.quote: the patient Sufferer treading the path to Calvary; the Prince of heaven hanging upon the cross; the haughty priests and the jeering rabble deriding His expiring agony; the supernatural darkness; the heaving earth, the rent rocks, the open graves, marking the moment when the world’s Redeemer yielded up His life.
claims.claim_text: Jesus Christ was crucified at Calvary amid mocking by priests and onlookers, and His death was accompanied by darkness, an earthquake, split rocks, and opened graves.

claims.quote: Herod, who slew the innocent children of Bethlehem that he might destroy the King of Israel
claims.claim_text: Herod ordered the killing of the innocent children of Bethlehem in an attempt to destroy the infant King of Israel (the Massacre of the Innocents).

claims.quote: the base Herodias, upon whose guilty soul rests the blood of John the Baptist
claims.claim_text: Herodias bears responsibility for the death of John the Baptist.

claims.quote: the priests and rulers and the maddened throng who cried, “His blood be on us, and on our children!”
claims.claim_text: The priests, rulers, and crowd at Jesus' trial are described as having cried 'His blood be on us, and on our children,' echoing Matthew 27:25.

claims.quote: Amid the ransomed throng are the apostles of Christ, the heroic Paul, the ardent Peter, the loved and loving John, and their true-hearted brethren
claims.claim_text: Paul, Peter, and John were apostles of Christ.

claims.quote: There is Nero, that monster of cruelty and vice, beholding the joy and exaltation of those whom he once tortured, and in whose extremest anguish he found satanic delight.
claims.claim_text: The Roman emperor Nero tortured and persecuted people (including Christians among his victims) and took delight in their suffering.

claims.quote: His mother is there to witness the result of her own work; to see how the evil stamp of character transmitted to her son, the passions encouraged and developed by her influence and example, have borne fruit in crimes that caused the world to shudder.
claims.claim_text: Nero's mother's character and influence contributed to shaping the cruel character for which Nero became known.

claims.quote: There are papist priests and prelates, who claimed to be Christ’s ambassadors, yet employed the rack, the dungeon, and the stake to control the consciences of His people.
claims.claim_text: Catholic priests and prelates who claimed to represent Christ used torture (the rack), imprisonment, and burning at the stake to control people's religious consciences.

claims.quote: There are the proud pontiffs who exalted themselves above God, and presumed to change the law of the Most High.
claims.claim_text: Sweeping interpretive characterization: Catholic popes exalted themselves above God and presumed to alter the law of God.

claims.quote: they feel the force of His own words, “Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these My brethren, ye have done it unto Me.”(1160)
claims.claim_text: Christ is quoted (footnote 1160) as having said, 'Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these My brethren, ye have done it unto Me' (cf. Matthew 25:40).

claims.quote: The whole wicked world stand arraigned at the bar of God, on the charge of high treason against the government of heaven.
claims.claim_text: According to White's theology, the whole wicked world is judged and condemned for treason against the government of heaven.

claims.quote: By their lives they have declared, “We will not have this Jesus to reign over us.”
claims.claim_text: The wicked's lives are characterized as declaring 'We will not have this Jesus to reign over us,' echoing Luke 19:14.

claims.quote: All see that their exclusion from heaven is just.
claims.claim_text: According to White, it becomes evident to all present that the wicked's exclusion from heaven is just.

claims.quote: all with one voice exclaim, “Great and marvelous are Thy works, Lord God Almighty; just and true are Thy ways, Thou King of saints;”(1161)
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted (footnote 1161): 'Great and marvelous are Thy works, Lord God Almighty; just and true are Thy ways, Thou King of saints' (cf. Revelation 15:3).

claims.quote: He who was once a covering cherub remembers whence he has fallen. A shining seraph, “son of the morning;” how changed, how degraded!
claims.claim_text: According to White's theology, Satan was originally 'a covering cherub' and a shining seraph called 'son of the morning' before his fall.

claims.quote: Memory recalls the home of his innocence and purity, the peace and content that were his until he indulged in murmuring against God, and envy of Christ.
claims.claim_text: According to White's theology, Satan's rebellion originated in murmuring against God and envy of Christ.

claims.quote: He reviews his work among men and its results,—the enmity of man toward his fellow-man, the terrible destruction of life, the rise and fall of kingdoms, the overturning of thrones, the long succession of tumults, conflicts, and revolutions.
claims.claim_text: White attributes to Satan's influence the enmity among humans, destruction of life, and the historical rise and fall of kingdoms, thrones, and revolutions.

claims.quote: He has worked deliberately and systematically, and with marvelous success, leading vast multitudes to accept his version of the great controversy which has been so long in progress. For thousands of years this chief of conspiracy has palmed off falsehood for truth.
claims.claim_text: According to White, for thousands of years Satan has deliberately worked to lead multitudes to accept a false account of the great controversy between good and evil.

claims.quote: And now Satan bows down, and confesses the justice of his sentence.
claims.claim_text: In White's narrative, Satan ultimately bows down and confesses the justice of God's sentence against him.

claims.quote: “Who shall not fear Thee, O Lord, and glorify Thy name? for Thou only art holy: for all nations shall come and worship before Thee; for Thy judgments are made manifest.”(1162)
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted (footnote 1162): 'Who shall not fear Thee, O Lord, and glorify Thy name? for Thou only art holy: for all nations shall come and worship before Thee; for Thy judgments are made manifest' (cf. Revelation 15:4).

claims.quote: “All Thy works shall praise Thee, O Lord; and Thy saints shall bless Thee.”(1163)
claims.claim_text: Psalms is quoted (footnote 1163): 'All Thy works shall praise Thee, O Lord; and Thy saints shall bless Thee' (cf. Psalm 145:10).

claims.quote: God’s wisdom, His justice, and His goodness stand fully vindicated.
claims.claim_text: According to White, at the close of the great controversy God's wisdom, justice, and goodness are fully vindicated before the universe.

claims.quote: the song of praise ascends from the white-robed ones about the throne, “Worthy is the Lamb that was slain to receive power, and riches, and wisdom, and strength, and honor, and glory, and blessing.”(1164)
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted (footnote 1164): 'Worthy is the Lamb that was slain to receive power, and riches, and wisdom, and strength, and honor, and glory, and blessing' (cf. Revelation 5:12).

claims.quote: Notwithstanding that Satan has been constrained to acknowledge God’s justice, and to bow to the supremacy of Christ, his character remains unchanged.
claims.claim_text: According to White, despite acknowledging God's justice, Satan's character remains unchanged and he attempts one final rebellion.

claims.quote: Their rage is kindled against Satan and those who have been his agents in deception, and with the fury of demons they turn upon them.
claims.claim_text: In White's final scene, the wicked turn in rage against Satan and his agents rather than continuing to follow him.

claims.quote: Saith the Lord: “Because thou hast set thine heart as the heart of God; behold, therefore I will bring strangers upon thee, the terrible of the nations: and they shall draw their swords against the beauty of thy wisdom, and they shall defile thy brightness. They shall bring thee down to the pit.” “I will destroy thee, O covering cherub, from the midst of the stones of fire.... I will cast thee to the ground, I will lay thee before kings, that they may behold thee.... I will bring thee to ashes upon the earth in the sight of all them that behold thee.... Thou shalt be a terror, and never shalt thou be any more.”(1165)
claims.claim_text: Ezekiel is quoted (footnote 1165, attributed to 'the Lord') pronouncing judgment on the 'covering cherub,' declaring destruction and reduction to ashes 'never to be any more' (cf. Ezekiel 28:2-19).

claims.quote: “Every battle of the warrior is with confused noise, and garments rolled in blood; but this shall be with burning and fuel of fire.” “The indignation of the Lord is upon all nations, and His fury upon all their armies: He hath utterly destroyed them, He hath delivered them to the slaughter.” “Upon the wicked He shall rain quick burning coals, fire and brimstone, and a horrible tempest: this shall be the portion of their cup.”(1166)
claims.claim_text: Scripture is quoted (footnote 1166, cf. Isaiah 9:5, Isaiah 34:2, Psalm 11:6) describing the final destruction of the wicked by fire and brimstone.

claims.quote: The elements melt with fervent heat, the earth also, and the works that are therein are burned up.(1167)
claims.claim_text: The text (footnote 1167) echoes 2 Peter 3:10 in describing the elements and earth melting with fervent heat and being burned up.

claims.quote: It is the time of the judgment and perdition of ungodly men,—“the day of the Lord’s vengeance, and the year of recompenses for the controversy of Zion.”(1168)
claims.claim_text: Isaiah is quoted (footnote 1168): 'the day of the Lord's vengeance, and the year of recompenses for the controversy of Zion' (cf. Isaiah 34:8).

claims.quote: Fire comes down from God out of heaven. The earth is broken up. The weapons concealed in its depths are drawn forth. Devouring flames burst from every yawning chasm. The very rocks are on fire.
claims.claim_text: In White's final judgment scene, fire descends from heaven, the earth's crust is broken up, and devouring flames burst forth destroying the wicked.

claims.quote: They “shall be stubble: and the day that cometh shall burn them up, saith the Lord of hosts.”(1170)
claims.claim_text: Malachi is quoted (footnote 1170): 'they shall be stubble: and the day that cometh shall burn them up, saith the Lord of hosts' (cf. Malachi 4:1).

claims.quote: Some are destroyed as in a moment, while others suffer many days. All are punished “according to their deeds.”
claims.claim_text: According to White, the wicked's final destruction varies in duration — some perish instantly, others suffer over many days — with punishment proportioned to their deeds.

claims.quote: The sins of the righteous having been transferred to Satan, he is made to suffer not only for his own rebellion, but for all the sins which he has caused God’s people to commit. His punishment is to be far greater than that of those whom he has deceived.
claims.claim_text: According to White's theology, Satan's final punishment exceeds that of the deceived wicked because the sins of the righteous, transferred to him, are added to his own rebellion.

claims.quote: For six thousand years he has wrought his will, filling the earth with woe, and causing grief throughout the universe.
claims.claim_text: Satan worked his will on earth for six thousand years, filling it with woe, prior to his final destruction.
claims.extractor_note: Chronological claim reflecting a young-earth biblical chronology (roughly 6,000 years from creation/the Fall to the eschaton); flagged as a checkable date/statistic claim per extraction rules even though it appears within theological narrative.

claims.quote: “The whole earth is at rest, and is quiet: they [the righteous] break forth into singing.”(1171)
claims.claim_text: Isaiah is quoted (footnote 1171): 'The whole earth is at rest, and is quiet: they break forth into singing' (cf. Isaiah 14:7).

claims.quote: “The voice of a great multitude,” “as the voice of many waters, and as the voice of mighty thunderings,” is heard, saying, “Alleluia; for the Lord God omnipotent reigneth.”
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted describing 'the voice of a great multitude...as the voice of many waters, and as the voice of mighty thunderings' proclaiming 'Alleluia; for the Lord God omnipotent reigneth' (cf. Revelation 19:6).

claims.quote: While the earth was wrapped in the fire of destruction, the righteous abode safely in the holy city. Upon those that had part in the first resurrection, the second death has no power.
claims.claim_text: According to White's theology, the righteous, having taken part in the first resurrection, remain safe in the holy city while the earth burns, and the second death has no power over them.

claims.quote: “I saw a new heaven and a new earth: for the first heaven and the first earth were passed away.”(1173)
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted (footnote 1173): 'I saw a new heaven and a new earth: for the first heaven and the first earth were passed away' (cf. Revelation 21:1).

claims.quote: No eternally burning hell will keep before the ransomed the fearful consequences of sin.
claims.claim_text: According to White's theology, no eternally burning hell exists to perpetually remind the redeemed of sin's consequences.

claims.quote: One reminder alone remains: our Redeemer will ever bear the marks of His crucifixion. Upon His wounded head, upon His side, His hands and feet, are the only traces of the cruel work that sin has wrought.
claims.claim_text: According to White's theology, Christ will eternally bear the wound marks of His crucifixion (head, side, hands, feet) as the sole remaining reminder of sin.

claims.quote: Says the prophet, beholding Christ in His glory, “He had bright beams coming out of His side: and there was the hiding of His power.”(1174)
claims.claim_text: The prophet Habakkuk is quoted (footnote 1174) describing Christ: 'He had bright beams coming out of His side: and there was the hiding of His power' (cf. Habakkuk 3:4).

claims.quote: “O tower of the flock, the stronghold of the daughter of Zion, unto thee shall it come, even the first dominion.”(1175)
claims.claim_text: Micah is quoted (footnote 1175): 'O tower of the flock, the stronghold of the daughter of Zion, unto thee shall it come, even the first dominion' (cf. Micah 4:8).

claims.quote: the time for “the redemption of the purchased possession.”(1176)
claims.claim_text: The text quotes (footnote 1176) 'the redemption of the purchased possession,' echoing Ephesians 1:14.

claims.quote: “Thus saith the Lord ... that formed the earth and made it; He hath established it, He created it not in vain, He formed it to be inhabited.”(1177)
claims.claim_text: Isaiah is quoted (footnote 1177): 'Thus saith the Lord...that formed the earth and made it; He hath established it, He created it not in vain, He formed it to be inhabited' (cf. Isaiah 45:18).

claims.quote: “The righteous shall inherit the land, and dwell therein forever.”(1178)
claims.claim_text: Psalms is quoted (footnote 1178): 'The righteous shall inherit the land, and dwell therein forever' (cf. Psalm 37:29).

claims.quote: The earth originally given to man as his kingdom, betrayed by him into the hands of Satan, and so long held by the mighty foe, has been brought back by the great plan of redemption. All that was lost by sin has been restored.
claims.claim_text: According to White's theology, the earth, originally given to man but forfeited to Satan through sin, is restored to the redeemed through the plan of redemption.

claims.quote: Christ assured His disciples that He went to prepare mansions for them in the Father’s house.
claims.claim_text: Jesus Christ told His disciples that He was going to prepare mansions (dwelling places) for them in His Father's house (cf. John 14:2).

claims.quote: “eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man, the things which God hath prepared for them that love Him.”(1179)
claims.claim_text: Paul is quoted (footnote 1179, cf. 1 Corinthians 2:9): 'eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man, the things which God hath prepared for them that love Him.'

claims.quote: In the Bible the inheritance of the saved is called a country.(1180)
claims.claim_text: The Bible refers to the inheritance of the saved as 'a country' (footnote 1180, cf. Hebrews 11:14-16).

claims.quote: The tree of life yields its fruit every month, and the leaves of the tree are for the service of the nations.
claims.claim_text: According to White's description of the new earth (echoing Revelation 22:2), the tree of life yields fruit monthly and its leaves serve the nations.

claims.quote: “My people shall dwell in a peaceful habitation, and in sure dwellings, and in quiet resting-places.” “Violence shall no more be heard in thy land, wasting nor destruction within thy borders; but thou shalt call thy walls Salvation, and thy gates Praise.” “They shall build houses, and inhabit them; and they shall plant vineyards, and eat the fruit of them, They shall not build, and another inhabit; they shalt not plant, and another eat:... Mine elect shall long enjoy the work of their hands.”(1181)
claims.claim_text: Isaiah is quoted (footnote 1181, cf. Isaiah 32:18, 60:18, 65:21-22) describing the redeemed dwelling securely and building houses and planting vineyards which they themselves enjoy.

claims.quote: “the wilderness and the solitary place shall be glad for them; and the desert shall rejoice, and blossom as the rose.” “Instead of the thorn shall come up the fir-tree, and instead of the brier shall come up the myrtle-tree.”(1182)
claims.claim_text: Isaiah is quoted (footnote 1182, cf. Isaiah 35:1, 55:13) describing the wilderness blossoming and thorns replaced by fir and myrtle trees in the new earth.

claims.quote: “The wolf also shall dwell with the lamb, and the leopard shall lie down with the kid; ... and a little child shall lead them.” “They shall not hurt nor destroy in all My holy mountain,”(1183)
claims.claim_text: Isaiah is quoted (footnote 1183, cf. Isaiah 11:6, 9) describing the wolf dwelling with the lamb and a little child leading them, with no hurt or destruction in God's holy mountain.

claims.quote: “There shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, ... for the former things are passed away.”(1184)
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted (footnote 1184): 'There shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying...for the former things are passed away' (cf. Revelation 21:4).

claims.quote: “The inhabitant shall not say, I am sick: the people that dwell therein shall be forgiven their iniquity.”(1185)
claims.claim_text: Isaiah is quoted (footnote 1185): 'The inhabitant shall not say, I am sick: the people that dwell therein shall be forgiven their iniquity' (cf. Isaiah 33:24).

claims.quote: “a crown of glory in the hand of the Lord, and a royal diadem in the hand of thy God.”(1186)
claims.claim_text: Isaiah is quoted (footnote 1186) describing the New Jerusalem as 'a crown of glory in the hand of the Lord, and a royal diadem in the hand of thy God' (cf. Isaiah 62:3).

claims.quote: “Her light was like unto a stone most precious, even like a jasper stone, clear as crystal.” “The nations of them which are saved shall walk in the light of it: and the kings of the earth do bring their glory and honor into it.”(1187)
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted (footnote 1187, cf. Revelation 21:11, 24) describing the New Jerusalem's light as like a jasper stone, with the saved nations walking in its light.

claims.quote: Saith the Lord, “I will rejoice in Jerusalem, and joy in My people.”(1188)
claims.claim_text: Isaiah is quoted (footnote 1188, attributed to 'the Lord'): 'I will rejoice in Jerusalem, and joy in My people' (cf. Isaiah 65:19).

claims.quote: “The tabernacle of God is with men, and He will dwell with them, and they shall be His people, and God Himself shall be with them, and be their God.”(1189)
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted (footnote 1189): 'The tabernacle of God is with men, and He will dwell with them, and they shall be His people, and God Himself shall be with them, and be their God' (cf. Revelation 21:3).

claims.quote: In the city of God “there shall be no night.”
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted describing the city of God: 'there shall be no night' (cf. Revelation 21:25, 22:5).

claims.quote: “And they need no candle, neither light of the sun; for the Lord God giveth them light.”(1190)
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted (footnote 1190): 'they need no candle, neither light of the sun; for the Lord God giveth them light' (cf. Revelation 22:5).

claims.quote: “I saw no temple therein: for the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are the temple of it.”(1191)
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted (footnote 1191): 'I saw no temple therein: for the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are the temple of it' (cf. Revelation 21:22).

claims.quote: “Now we see through a glass, darkly.”(1192)
claims.claim_text: Paul is quoted (footnote 1192, cf. 1 Corinthians 13:12): 'Now we see through a glass, darkly.'

claims.quote: There the redeemed shall “know, even as also they are known.”
claims.claim_text: Paul is quoted (cf. 1 Corinthians 13:12): the redeemed 'shall know, even as also they are known.'

claims.quote: the sacred ties that bind together “the whole family in heaven and earth,”(1193)
claims.claim_text: The text quotes (footnote 1193) 'the whole family in heaven and earth,' echoing Ephesians 3:15.

claims.quote: Every faculty will be developed, every capacity increased. The acquirement of knowledge will not weary the mind or exhaust the energies.
claims.claim_text: According to White's theology, in the new earth the redeemed will experience continual growth of faculties and capacities without mental fatigue.

claims.quote: Unfettered by mortality, they wing their tireless flight to worlds afar,—worlds that thrilled with sorrow at the spectacle of human woe, and rang with songs of gladness at the tidings of a ransomed soul.
claims.claim_text: According to White's theology, the redeemed will travel unhindered to other worlds in the universe, worlds described as having responded with sorrow and joy to events on earth.

claims.quote: As Jesus opens before them the riches of redemption, and the amazing achievements in the great controversy with Satan, the hearts of the ransomed thrill with more fervent devotion, and with more rapturous joy they sweep the harps of gold
claims.claim_text: According to White's theology, throughout eternity Christ will progressively reveal to the redeemed more of the riches of redemption and the events of the great controversy with Satan.

claims.quote: “And every creature which is in heaven, and on the earth, and under the earth, and such as are in the sea, and all that are in them, heard I saying, Blessing, and honor, and glory, and power, be unto Him that sitteth upon the throne, and unto the Lamb forever and ever.”(1194)
claims.claim_text: Revelation is quoted (footnote 1194): every creature in heaven, on earth, under the earth, and in the sea is heard ascribing blessing, honor, glory, and power to Him on the throne and to the Lamb forever (cf. Revelation 5:13).

claims.quote: The great controversy is ended. Sin and sinners are no more. The entire universe is clean.
claims.claim_text: According to White's theology, the great controversy between good and evil ends with sin and sinners eliminated and the entire universe cleansed.

claims.quote: From the minutest atom to the greatest world, all things, animate and inanimate, in their unshadowed beauty and perfect joy, declare that God is love.
claims.claim_text: The book's concluding interpretive claim: the harmony of the restored universe, from the smallest to the largest, testifies that God is love.
